name: Garganta de las Nogaleas Trail
name_original: Valle del Jerte – ruta circular por la Garganta de las Nogaleas
description: Hike the easy 3.1-mile Garganta de las Nogaleas Trail in Valle del Jerte, featuring stunning waterfalls and panoramic views.
region_intro: Hiking around Navaconcejo offers access to the Jerte Valley, a region characterized by its river gorges, extensive cherry orchards, and diverse forest types including oak and chestnut. The area features numerous waterfalls and natural pools, notably within the Garganta de los Infiernos Natural Reserve. Trails traverse varied terrain, from riverbanks to higher elevations within the Gredos massif, providing a range of hiking experiences. Best hiking trails around Navaconcejo The most popular hiking route is Valle del Jerte - circular route through the Garganta de las Nogaleas, a 3.1 miles (5.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 45 minutes to complete. This easy route explores the Nogaleas Gorge with its water features. Another top favourite among local hikers is Cascada del Caozo – circular from Valdastillas (PR-CC 32), a moderate 5.1 miles (8.2 km) path. This trail leads to the Cascada del Caozo waterfall through varied forest and river landscapes. Local hikers also love the Nogaleas Waterfalls – Panoramic view of the Jerte Valley loop from Rebollar, a 6.6 miles (10.6 km) trail leading through the Nogaleas Waterfalls area and offering expansive views of the Jerte Valley, often completed in about 3 hours 17 minutes. Hiking around Navaconcejo is defined by river valleys, waterfalls, and diverse forests, with trails ranging from gentle riverside paths to more challenging ascents into the Gredos massif. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to moderate hikes. The routes in Navaconcejo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 350 reviews. More than 2000 hikers have used komoot to explore Navaconcejo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Navaconcejo?
A: Navaconcejo offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with nearly 100 trails documented on komoot. These routes cater to different skill levels, from easy strolls along the river to more challenging ascents into the Gredos massif.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for families in Navaconcejo?
A: Yes, Navaconcejo has several easy trails perfect for families. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990319686/valle-del-jerte-circular-route-through-the-garganta-de-las-nogaleas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valle del Jerte - circular route through the Garganta de las Nogaleas&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 5 km long and explores the beautiful Nogaleas Gorge with its water features. There are 25 easy routes in total, ensuring plenty of choices for a relaxed family outing.

Q: Can I find trails with waterfalls near Navaconcejo?
A: Absolutely! The region is famous for its waterfalls. You can explore the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4204503" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nogaleas Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt; via routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38223820" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nogaleas Waterfalls – Panoramic view of the Jerte Valley loop from Rebollar&lt;/a&gt;. Another popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990727089/cascada-del-caozo-circular-from-valdastillas-pr-cc-32" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada del Caozo – circular from Valdastillas (PR-CC 32)&lt;/a&gt;, which leads to the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4360769" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caozo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the Navaconcejo area?
A: Yes, many trails in Navaconcejo are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990319686/valle-del-jerte-circular-route-through-the-garganta-de-las-nogaleas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valle del Jerte - circular route through the Garganta de las Nogaleas&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990727089/cascada-del-caozo-circular-from-valdastillas-pr-cc-32" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada del Caozo – circular from Valdastillas (PR-CC 32)&lt;/a&gt;. These loops are ideal for exploring the diverse landscapes without retracing your steps.

Q: What kind of natural landscapes and attractions can I expect to see on a hike?
A: Hiking around Navaconcejo immerses you in the natural beauty of the Jerte Valley. You'll encounter diverse terrain, from lush oak and chestnut forests to river gorges and higher elevations within the Gredos massif. Key attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4204503" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nogaleas Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt;, the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3243264" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Pilones Natural Pools&lt;/a&gt; in Garganta de los Infiernos, and the flowing &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3287752" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jerte River&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any trails that offer panoramic views of the Jerte Valley?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its stunning vistas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38223820" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nogaleas Waterfalls – Panoramic view of the Jerte Valley loop from Rebollar&lt;/a&gt; is a great option, offering expansive views. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5638307" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorrero de la Virgen Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; provides another excellent spot to take in the valley's beauty.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Navaconcejo?
A: The Jerte Valley is beautiful year-round, but specific seasons offer unique experiences. March and April are ideal for witnessing the famous cherry blossoms, while autumn (around November) brings spectacular golden foliage. During hotter months, trails through wooded areas provide welcome shade.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails around Navaconcejo?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many hiking trails in the Navaconcejo area, especially in natural parks and public lands. However, it's always recommended to keep them on a leash, especially in protected areas like the Garganta de los Infiernos Natural Reserve, and to respect local regulations regarding wildlife and other hikers.

Q: Are there any natural swimming spots accessible from hiking trails?
A: Yes, the Jerte River, which flows through Navaconcejo, offers several natural bathing areas. The most famous are "La Mora" and "El Pilar." Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3243264" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Pilones Natural Pools&lt;/a&gt; within the Garganta de los Infiernos Natural Reserve are iconic for their crystal-clear waters, perfect for a refreshing dip after a hike.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Navaconcejo?
A: The trails in Navaconcejo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 350 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the abundance of waterfalls, and the well-maintained paths that lead through charming forests and along the river.

Q: Are there options for more challenging or advanced hikes?
A: While Navaconcejo offers many easy and moderate routes, there are also options for more demanding hikes. The region's proximity to the Gredos massif means some trails involve greater elevation gain and more rugged terrain, providing a challenge for experienced hikers. There are 13 difficult routes available for those seeking a more strenuous adventure.

Q: Is parking available at the trailheads in Navaconcejo?
A: Yes, many popular trailheads around Navaconcejo and the wider Jerte Valley offer designated parking areas. It's common to find parking facilities near the starting points of well-known routes, especially those leading to attractions like the Nogaleas Waterfalls or the Garganta de los Infiernos. However, during peak seasons, these can fill up quickly.
tips: This is one of the most popular and recognized tours in the province of Cáceres. It is an itinerary with 6 waterfalls and several viewpoints with very little technical difficulty, ideal to do with the whole family, even with the little ones. Highly recommended to go in the spring or summer months; the wooded path will give you very good shade and the waters of the chorreras are very refreshing.

faqs.question: Where does the Garganta de las Nogaleas Trail start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The trail typically starts in the town of Navaconcejo, Cáceres, Spain. You'll find parking available in or near the town, which serves as the main access point for the route.

faqs.question: Is the Garganta de las Nogaleas Trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: While often described as generally easy, the trail involves a short but intense climb with a significant elevation gain. There are many sections with stairs and some irregular footing. It's manageable for beginners with a reasonable level of fitness, but be prepared for a continuous uphill ascent.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail begins on a paved or cement path before transitioning into a path through dense vegetation along the gorge. The ascent features many sections with stone and wooden steps, and handrails in more exposed areas. The return journey often follows a paved track through cherry orchards.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The best times to visit are during winter and spring when the water flow in the waterfalls is abundant due to snowmelt and spring rains. Late March and early April are particularly breathtaking due to the cherry blossom season in the Jerte Valley.

faqs.question: What are the main attractions I'll see along the route?
faqs.answer: The primary attraction is the succession of impressive waterfalls and natural pools, including the famous Nogaleas Waterfalls. You'll also enjoy lush riverside vegetation, and panoramic views of the Jerte Valley and its cherry terraces as you ascend.

faqs.question: Is the trail a loop or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: The Garganta de las Nogaleas Trail is generally a circular route, offering a varied experience as you ascend alongside the gorge and descend through orchards. Linear options are also possible if you prefer.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the Garganta de las Nogaleas Trail or the Valle del Jerte?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no permits or entrance fees required to hike the Garganta de las Nogaleas Trail or to access the general area of the Valle del Jerte.

faqs.question: Is the Garganta de las Nogaleas Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, due to the presence of stairs, handrails, and potentially crowded sections, it's advisable to keep your dog on a leash and ensure they are comfortable with varied terrain.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the trail?
faqs.answer: The full circular route usually takes around 2 to 3 hours to complete, depending on your pace and how much time you spend enjoying the waterfalls and views.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route is primarily the "Ruta Garganta de las Nogaleas" itself. However, sections of the trail also pass through parts of the "Valle del Jerte" and the "Avenida de la Virgen del Pilar" routes.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: We recommend bringing sturdy hiking shoes suitable for varied terrain, water, and snacks. Depending on the season, a light jacket or rain gear might be useful. Don't forget your camera to capture the beautiful waterfalls and valley views!
